text: Agile methodology is a flexible approach to project management, especially common in software development. Instead of trying to finish everything at once, work is divided into short cycles called sprints, each lasting a few weeks. The team keeps a backlog, a list of tasks that need to be done. Each task in the backlog is described as a user story, focusing on what a user needs, for example: "As a user, I want to log in quickly." Every morning, the team holds a short standup meeting to share progress and plan the day. This allows for iterative development, meaning the team continuously improves the product based on feedback after each sprint.
